facts.question: What is Python?
facts.answer: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its simplicity and readability

facts.question: What is Java?
facts.answer: Java is an object-oriented programming language designed for platform independence through the Java Virtual Machine

facts.question: What is machine learning?
facts.answer: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed

facts.question: What is the speed of light?
facts.answer: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second

facts.question: What is the atomic number of Oxygen?
facts.answer: Oxygen has an atomic number of 8

facts.question: What is the highest mountain in the world?
facts.answer: Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world with a height of 8,849 meters

facts.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
facts.answer: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system

facts.question: What is SRKR Engineering College?
facts.answer: Sagi Rama Krishnam Raju Engineering College is an autonomous institution affiliated to JNTUK, Kakinada, approved by AICTE and accredited by NAAC with A+ grade. These regulations apply to B.Tech students admitted from the academic year 2023-24 onwards.

facts.question: What is the duration of B.Tech programme at SRKR?
facts.answer: The B.Tech regular programme duration is four academic years and shall not exceed eight academic years. Students availing the gap year facility may extend the duration by a maximum of two additional years.

facts.question: How many credits are required for B.Tech degree at SRKR?
facts.answer: To be eligible for the award of the B.Tech degree, a student must register for and successfully secure a total of 160 credits as per the prescribed curriculum structure.

facts.question: What are the requirements for Honors or Minor at SRKR?
facts.answer: B.Tech with Honors or B.Tech with Minor will be awarded if a student earns an additional 18 credits beyond the regular programme requirements. Registration for Honors or Minors is optional.

facts.question: What is the admission policy for B.Tech at SRKR?
facts.answer: Admissions to the B.Tech programme are made based on eligibility criteria and merit ranks obtained in entrance examinations conducted by the Government or University, subject to reservation rules prescribed from time to time.

facts.question: What is the definition of one credit at SRKR?
facts.answer: One credit is equivalent to one hour of lecture or tutorial per week or two hours of practical or laboratory work per week. Credits quantify the workload required for successful completion of a course.

facts.question: What is the semester structure at SRKR?
facts.answer: Each academic year consists of two semesters. Each semester comprises a minimum of 90 working days including examinations.

facts.question: What is the B.Tech programme structure at SRKR?
facts.answer: The undergraduate programme includes Humanities and Management, Basic Sciences, Engineering Sciences, Professional Core courses, Electives, Internships, Project work, and Mandatory non-credit courses totaling 160 credits.

facts.question: How many elective courses are available at SRKR?
facts.answer: The curriculum provides increased flexibility with five professional elective courses and four open elective courses, allowing students to specialize in emerging areas of their discipline.

facts.question: What is the student induction programme at SRKR?
facts.answer: A mandatory three-week student induction programme is conducted for first-year students before the commencement of the first semester as per AICTE guidelines.

facts.question: What are the attendance requirements at SRKR?
facts.answer: Students must maintain at least 40 percent attendance in each course and 75 percent attendance in aggregate to be eligible for semester end examinations. Attendance below 65 percent shall not be condoned.

facts.question: Can attendance shortage be condoned at SRKR?
facts.answer: Condonation of attendance shortage up to 10 percent may be granted by the College Academic Committee subject to payment of the prescribed fee.

facts.question: How are theory courses evaluated at SRKR?
facts.answer: Theory courses are evaluated for 100 marks consisting of 30 marks for Continuous Internal Evaluation and 70 marks for Semester End Examination.

facts.question: How is internal evaluation calculated at SRKR?
facts.answer: Continuous Internal Evaluation includes two internal exams comprising objective questions, subjective questions, and assignments. Final internal marks are calculated using 80 percent weightage of the better exam and 20 percent of the other.

facts.question: What are the minimum pass marks at SRKR?
facts.answer: A student must secure a minimum of 35 percent marks in the semester end examination and 40 percent in total to pass a theory or practical course.

facts.question: How are practical courses evaluated at SRKR?
facts.answer: Practical courses are evaluated with 30 marks for internal assessment and 70 marks for end examination, including procedure, experiment results, and viva voce.

facts.question: What are the mandatory non-credit courses at SRKR?
facts.answer: Mandatory non-credit courses such as Environmental Science and Indian Constitution are evaluated internally and must be passed to qualify for degree completion.

facts.question: What are skill enhancement courses at SRKR?
facts.answer: Five skill enhancement courses are offered between the third and seventh semesters, including domain-specific, interdisciplinary, and soft skill courses.

facts.question: What are the MOOC requirements at SRKR?
facts.answer: Students must successfully complete at least one approved MOOC course during the programme. Core courses are not permitted through MOOCs.

facts.question: What is the credit transfer policy at SRKR?
facts.answer: Credit transfer through MOOCs is permitted up to a maximum of 20 percent of total programme credits, applicable only to professional and open elective courses.

facts.question: What are the summer internship requirements at SRKR?
facts.answer: Two mandatory summer internships of minimum eight weeks each must be completed at the end of second and third years, focusing on community service and industry exposure respectively.

facts.question: What is the final year project requirement at SRKR?
facts.answer: In the final semester, students must undergo a full semester internship along with project work, which is evaluated for a total of 200 marks.

facts.question: What is the revaluation policy at SRKR?
facts.answer: Revaluation is permitted only for theory course semester end examinations. A third valuation is conducted if the difference exceeds 20 percent.

facts.question: What grading system does SRKR use?
facts.answer: The institution follows a 10-point absolute grading system ranging from S grade for 90 percent and above to F grade for marks below 40. Absent students receive Ab grade.

facts.question: What are the promotion rules at SRKR?
facts.answer: Promotion to higher semesters requires satisfying attendance criteria and securing at least 40 percent of credits in completed semesters.

facts.question: What are the malpractice consequences at SRKR?
facts.answer: Malpractice during examinations including copying, impersonation, possession of prohibited material, or misconduct leads to cancellation of performance, debarment, or forfeiture of seat.

facts.question: What is the medium of instruction at SRKR?
facts.answer: The medium of instruction for the entire B.Tech programme including examinations and project reports shall be English only.
